2605.07883.title: Beyond "I cannot fulfill this request": Alleviating Rigid Rejection in LLMs via Label Enhancement
2605.07883.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) rely on safety alignment to obey safe requests while refusing harmful ones. However, traditional refusal mechanisms often lead to "rigid rejection," where a general template (e.g., "I cannot fulfill this request") indiscriminately triggers refusals and severely undermines the naturalness of interactions between humans and LLMs. To address this issue, LANCE is proposed in this paper to ensure safe yet flexible and natural responses via label enhancement. Specifically, LANCE employs variational inference to perform label enhancement, predicting a continuous distribution across multiple rejection categories. These fine-grained rejection distributions provide multi-way textual gradients for a refinement model to neutralize the hazardous elements in the prompt, so that the LLMs could generate safe responses that avoid rigid rejections while preserving the naturalness of interactions. Experiments demonstrate that LANCE significantly alleviates the rigid rejection problem while maintaining high security standards, significantly outperforming existing baseline models in terms of helpfulness and naturalness of responses.
2605.07883.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）依賴安全對齊來遵循安全請求，同時拒絕有害請求。<lb/>然而，傳統的拒絕機制往往導致「僵硬拒絕」，即一個通用模板（例如，「我無法滿足這個請求」）不加區別地觸發拒絕，並嚴重削弱人類與LLMs之間互動的自然性。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，本文提出了LANCE，以確保通過標籤增強實現安全且靈活自然的回應。<lb/>具體而言，LANCE利用變分推斷進行標籤增強，預測多個拒絕類別之間的連續分佈。<lb/>這些細緻的拒絕分佈為精煉模型提供了多向文本梯度，以中和提示中的危險元素，從而使LLMs能夠生成安全的回應，避免僵硬拒絕，同時保持互動的自然性。<lb/>實驗表明，LANCE顯著減輕了僵硬拒絕問題，同時保持高安全標準，在回應的有用性和自然性方面顯著超越現有基準模型。
2605.07883.author: Ying Zhang et.al.
2605.07883.authors: Ying Zhang, Congyu Qiao, Xin Geng, Ning Xu
